title: hieracium bifidum subsp. microscapum
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. microscapum, also known as a micro-leaved hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its small, white to yellowish flowers and divided leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Hieracium bifidum subsp. microscapum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, teas, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
